description: Brazilian criminal organization founded in 2007 in Manaus, Amazonas, led by drug traffickers Zé Roberto da Compensa, João Pinto Carioca, and Gelson Carnaúba. It was one of the largest criminal groups in Amazonas, involved in drug trafficking, murders, robberies, extortion, and prison rebellions. The group engaged in violent conflicts with the Primeiro Comando da Capital (PCC) and later with the Comando Vermelho (CV), leading to its decline and eventual dissolution around 2021.

country_links.evidence_quote: Há fortes indícios de que a FDN faça parte de uma rede internacional de tráfico de drogas com fortes conexões com as FARC na Colômbia
country_links.context: The FDN is part of an international drug trafficking network with strong connections to the FARC in Colombia.

country_links.evidence_quote: Território(s): Amazonas , Acre , Pará , Rondônia , Ceará , além de Colômbia , Peru , Venezuela
country_links.context: The organization has territory in Peru.
